title: ophrys archipelagi subsp. cephalonica
intro: Ophrys archipelagi subsp. cephalonica, also known as the Cephalonian bee orchid, is a sub-species of the Ophrys archipelagi orchid, a type of flowering plant native to Greece. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ophrys archipelagi subsp. cephalonica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible antidiabetic and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
